hypothesis: At 85 original requests per second against 100 attempts per second of modeled capacity, three-attempt full-jitter retries under temporally correlated failures will more than double peak attempted load versus no retry; a shared retry budget will reduce that peak by at least 40% while retaining a higher completion rate than no retry.
claimBoundary: The artifacts support a retry-load mechanism and a completion-versus-capacity trade-off inside the declared discrete-time stochastic simulator. They do not establish a universal retry count, token-bucket rate, provider recovery curve, production completion rate, or applicability to non-idempotent operations.

baselines: no_retry on the same original-request arrival and exogenous-fault trace
exponential_no_jitter with the same three-attempt ceiling
controls: independent per-attempt faults matched to the correlated process stationary mean
design.pairing: Policies within a load, fault-shape, and repeat cell share original arrivals and the exogenous fault trace.
design.exploratory: Immediate retry and no-jitter comparisons diagnose synchronization and latency; the primary contrast is full jitter versus no retry and budgeted full jitter at 85 requests/s under correlated faults.

reproduction.runtime: Python 3 standard library; no Torch, model, accelerator, or network service
